label: Redshift
description: Query and analyze data in your Amazon Redshift data warehouse using standard SQL.

form.title: Connect Redshift
form.subtitle: Enter your Redshift cluster endpoint and credentials to connect Anton to your data warehouse.

methods.label: Cluster credentials
methods.description: Connect directly using your Redshift cluster endpoint, port, database name, and a database user and password. The simplest and most common path.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with cluster credentials

You need your cluster endpoint, the database name, and a database-level user/password.

### Finding your cluster endpoint

1. Open the [Amazon Redshift console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/redshiftv2/).
2. Click **Clusters** in the left nav, then click your cluster name.
3. On the **General information** tab, find **Endpoint**. It looks like:
   `my-cluster.abc123xyz.us-east-1.redshift.amazonaws.com:5439/mydb`
4. Copy the host part (everything before the colon), the port (default **5439**), and the database name (the segment after the last slash).

### Finding or creating a database user

1. Connect to Redshift with a superuser (e.g. via the Query Editor v2 in the console).
2. Run `CREATE USER anton PASSWORD 'StrongPassword123';` and grant the required schema/table permissions.
3. Use those credentials in the fields below.

### Network access

- Your Redshift cluster must be **publicly accessible**, or Anton must run inside the same VPC.
- Ensure the cluster's **security group inbound rules** allow TCP on port 5439 from Anton's IP.
- If your cluster is in a private subnet, configure a VPC endpoint or bastion tunnel before connecting.

### Notes

- Credentials are stored locally and never sent to Anton's servers.
- IAM authentication is not yet supported — use a native database user.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Cluster endpoint (host)
fields.description: The hostname portion of your cluster endpoint, found under Clusters → your cluster → General information in the Redshift console.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Redshift's default port is 5439. Change only if your cluster uses a custom port.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database name
fields.description: The name of the Redshift database you want to connect to (e.g. dev, analytics, prod).

fields.name: username
fields.label: Database user
fields.description: A Redshift database user with SELECT (and any other required) privileges on the target schemas and tables.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the database user above. Stored locally and never transmitted to Anton's servers.

fields.name: ssl
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: Enable SSL/TLS for the connection. Strongly recommended; disable only if your cluster explicitly does not support SSL.
